title: galium andrewsii subsp. andrewsii
intro: Galium andrewsii subsp. andrewsii, also known as Andrews' gallium or Andrews' bedstraw, is a subspecies of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the western United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestive health. Andrews' gallium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used by herbalists for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
